title: Two Nova Scotians Win Oscars for Production Design on Frankenstein
text: Two Nova Scotia residents claimed the Academy Award for production design at the 98th Academy Awards on March 15, 2026. Tamara Deverell (Cape Breton) won Production Designer and Shane Vieau (Dartmouth) won Set Decorator. The pair won for their work on Guillermo del Toro's adaptation of Mary Shelley's Frankenstein. Actors Sigourney Weaver and Pedro Pascal presented the award. This marked Deverell's first Oscar win. Vieau previously won an Academy Award in 2017 for his set decoration work on The Shape of Water, also directed by del Toro. During their acceptance speech, Deverell acknowledged those who supported them in Canada and the U.K. Frankenstein earned additional Oscars in makeup, hairstyling, and costume design categories. The film was a major success for both the filmmakers and the two Nova Scotia-based production professionals.
